originalTitle: Conservative Anglicans pull back from electing rival to Archbishop of Canterbury
title: Good News! Church Friends Decide to Stick Together!
content:
Hello, amazing kids of Africa! Your friendly news reporter is here with some super interesting news from around the world. Today, we're talking about churches and their leaders. Imagine if your school decided to have two principals! That would be a bit confusing, right? Well, something similar was almost going to happen with some churches, but they made a very kind decision!

What Was Going On?

Sometimes, people who go to the same church might have different ideas about how things should be done. It's like when you and your friends want to play different games – one wants to play football, and the other wants to draw. These grown-ups who go to a special church called the Anglican Church were having a big discussion. A group of these church friends, who like to do things in a very traditional way, were thinking about choosing their *own* big leader. This leader would be like a special 'super principal' just for their group. They were thinking about having a different main boss than the one many other Anglican churches already follow.

Who is the Main Church Boss?

The main boss for many Anglican churches all over the world is a very important person called the Archbishop of Canterbury. It's a long name, isn't it? Think of him like the captain of a big, big team, or the head teacher of all the Anglican schools in many different countries. He helps everyone work together and leads them in their faith. Did you know? The Archbishop of Canterbury lives in a country called England, but his work helps churches in places like Kenya, Nigeria, South Africa, and many other amazing countries right here in Africa!

Why Did They Think About a New Leader?

Well, sometimes, grown-ups in the church have different thoughts. One big reason for their discussions was about who can be a leader in the church. Recently, a wonderful lady named Sarah Mullally became a very important leader in the church. She's the first lady to have such a big job in that specific part of the church! Some people were very happy about this, seeing it as a new way to help everyone serve God. But the group we are talking about, who like things to stay very traditional, weren't so sure. They had different ideas about whether ladies should have such big leadership roles in the church. It's like how some people might like vanilla ice cream and others prefer chocolate – different tastes!

What Did They Decide?

Guess what? After a lot of talking and thinking, this group decided *not* to choose their own special 'super principal' leader. They decided to *pull back* from that idea. That means they changed their minds and chose not to go ahead with it! This is very good news! It means that even though they have some different ideas, they still want to try and work together with the bigger Anglican family. They want to find ways to talk and understand each other, instead of creating a separate group. Imagine if your friends had a disagreement, but then decided to still play together and talk it out. That's a bit like what happened here. It shows how important it is to try and get along, even when you don't always agree.

Why Is This Good News for Africa?

Many, many people in our beloved Africa are part of the Anglican Church. From bustling cities to peaceful villages, Anglican churches are places where people come together to pray, sing, and learn about God. When church leaders decide to work together, it helps make all these churches stronger and happier. It means more unity, which is like everyone holding hands and walking in the same direction. When leaders are united, they can focus on helping people, teaching about kindness, and spreading love. This is wonderful for our communities here in Africa! Did you know? Africa has some of the biggest Anglican churches in the world! Millions of people in countries like Nigeria and Uganda are Anglicans. So, decisions made by church leaders can affect many families right here!

Learning to Get Along

This story teaches us a very important lesson: even when people have different thoughts or ideas, it's always better to try and understand each other and work together. It's okay to disagree sometimes, but it's even better to find a way to stay friends and family. The leaders in this church group showed that they care about staying part of the bigger family. They didn't want to cause more separation. They want to keep talking and praying together, finding common ground. Did you know? The word 'Anglican' comes from the word 'Angle', which is an old name for England. But now, the Anglican Church is all over the world, not just in England!

What Happens Next?

Now, the journey continues for these church friends. They will keep talking, keep sharing their ideas, and keep trying to understand each other. It's a bit like a big family meeting where everyone gets to speak and listen. This decision helps everyone stay connected to the main leader, the Archbishop of Canterbury, and to all the other Anglican churches around the globe. It's a step towards more peace and togetherness in the big family of faith. We hope that all the church leaders can continue to work hand-in-hand, making our world a kinder and more peaceful place for everyone, especially for all you amazing children of Africa! Thank you for listening to your kid-friendly news report! Stay curious and keep smiling!
summary: A group of church leaders, called Anglicans, decided not to choose their own separate leader, even though they had some different ideas, choosing instead to work together with the main church boss.
whyMatters: This is important because it means church leaders are trying to stay united and work together, which helps churches in Africa and around the world be stronger and focus on helping people and spreading kindness, instead of being divided.

originalTitle: DR Congo government says 200 killed after landslide at rebel-held mine
title: Sad News from Our African Family: Earth Moved Too Fast in DR Congo!
content:
Hello, hello, amazing kids of Africa! Your friendly news reporter is here with an important update from a country very close to us, the Democratic Republic of Congo (DR Congo). Oh no, we have some very sad news to share today. Imagine a big hill, or even a small mountain, where people are working. Sometimes, the earth and rocks on that hill can move very, very fast! This is called a **landslide**. A big landslide happened in a place where people dig for special shiny rocks. We call these places **mines**. Many, many people were there, working hard, and sadly, when the earth moved so fast, some people got hurt, and some lost their lives. It's a very sad day for our brothers and sisters in DR Congo. The government in DR Congo has said that around 200 people might have been affected by this sudden event. That's a lot of people, like all the kids in many classrooms combined! It makes our hearts feel heavy. This sad accident happened in a part of DR Congo where people look for a very important shiny rock called 'coltan'. This rock is super special because it helps to make many things we use every day, like our mobile phones and computers, work! This area, where the mine is, is looked after by a group of people. We don't have all the answers from them yet about what happened. Everyone is trying to understand more and help those who are in need.

What is a Landslide?

Imagine you are building a big sandcastle on the beach. You make it tall and strong! But then, a very big wave comes and washes a part of it away very quickly. A landslide is a bit like that, but with real earth, mud, and big rocks on a hill or a mountain. It can happen when there's too much rain, and the ground gets super soft and heavy. Or sometimes, the ground underneath can shake, making everything unstable. When the earth can't hold itself up anymore, it just rushes down very fast. **Did you know?** Our Earth is always moving, but sometimes it moves too fast, like in a landslide, and it can cause big changes on the surface! It reminds us that nature can be very powerful.

What are Mines and Why Can They Be Dangerous?

People dig deep, deep into the earth to find special treasures like shiny rocks or special metals. These places are called mines. These rocks and metals, which we call **minerals**, are super important. They help us make so many things that make our lives easier and more fun! For example, some minerals help build cars, some make our phones work, and some even help us build tall buildings. So, the people who work in mines are doing a very important job for all of us. But digging deep into the earth can be very dangerous. The ground can be unstable, meaning it's not always strong enough to stay put. Big rocks can fall, or tunnels can collapse. That's why it's super, super important for mines to be built and managed in a way that is very, very safe for all the workers. **Did you know?** The Democratic Republic of Congo is like a treasure chest! It's rich in many special minerals like copper, cobalt, and coltan, which are used all over the world!

Who Are the Brave Helpers?

When something sad and big like this landslide happens, many brave people come rushing to help! These amazing people are called rescue workers. They work incredibly hard and sometimes risk their own safety to find people who might be trapped or hurt. Doctors and nurses are also there to help those who are injured and need medical care. They give medicines and make sure people feel better. It's like when your friend falls and scrapes their knee, and you help them up and get them a bandage! These helpers are like super-heroes in real life. Everyone works together, like a big team, to make sure that everyone affected gets the help and support they need during such a difficult time.

Why Is It Hard to Know Everything Right Away?

Sometimes, after a big accident like a landslide, it's very hard to count exactly how many people were affected, or to know every single detail right away. This is especially true in places that are far away from big cities, or in areas that are difficult to reach. It takes time for all the news and information to come out. Rescue workers need time to search, and officials need time to gather all the facts carefully. So, we wait patiently for more information to understand better what happened and how we can best help the families and people in DR Congo.

What Can We Learn from This Sad News?

This sad news from our friends in DR Congo reminds us how incredibly important safety is, especially for people who work in jobs that can be dangerous, like mining. We need to always think about how to make sure everyone can go to work and come home safely. It also reminds us to be kind and to think about people who are going through hard times. We can send our love, our good thoughts, and our prayers to the families who have lost loved ones or have been hurt in this accident in DR Congo. **Did you know?** Every day, clever engineers and scientists around the world are trying to find new and better ways to make mines even safer for all the workers! Let's hope everyone stays safe, and that the helpers can do their important work. We will keep you updated when we hear more news. Stay curious, stay kind, and remember, we are all one big African family!
summary: A sad landslide happened in a mine in the Democratic Republic of Congo, where many people were working to find special rocks, causing around 200 people to be affected, and many brave helpers are working to rescue and care for them.
whyMatters: This is important because it reminds us that safety is super important for people who work in dangerous places like mines, and it teaches us to be kind and think about helping people who are going through sad times, like our friends in DR Congo.

originalTitle: The devastating conflict where both sides have reasons to keep fighting
title: Sad News from Our Friends in Sudan
content:
Hello, my wonderful friends! Your friend, Aisha, is here with some news from our amazing continent, Africa. Today, we're going to talk about a country called Sudan. It's a very big country in Africa, with lots of kind people and beautiful places. But right now, there's some sad news happening there. It's a bit tricky to understand, but I'll tell you about it in a way that helps us all learn and care.

What is Happening in Sudan?

Imagine if two groups of grown-ups just couldn't agree on important things. Sometimes, when grown-ups can't agree, they start to argue very, very much. In Sudan, this argument has become very big and very sad. It's like a big fight, and we call it a 'conflict.' This conflict means that some people are fighting each other. It's not a game, and it's not fun. When there is fighting, people can get hurt, and homes can be broken. This makes many children and families in Sudan very scared and very sad. They just want peace, which means no more fighting. Did you know? Sudan is the third-largest country in Africa! It has a very famous river called the River Nile flowing through it.

Why is the Fighting Continuing?

This big fight in Sudan has been going on for a little while now. One reason it's hard to stop is because the grown-ups involved have fought before. It's like if you had an argument with a friend a long time ago, and then you argued again – sometimes it feels harder to make up because of the old arguments. Sudan has a history of these big arguments, making it really tough to find a way to stop this time. Also, another sad thing is that the people fighting have many 'weapons.' Weapons are things that can hurt people, like guns. When there are lots of weapons, it makes it easier for the fighting to keep going on. If there were no weapons, it would be much harder for them to fight like this. It's like if you had lots of toys for a pretend fight; it makes the game last longer. But this is not a game. Did you know? The capital city of Sudan is called Khartoum. It's where the Blue Nile and White Nile rivers meet!

How Does This Make Life Hard for Children?

When there is fighting in a country, many things that we think are normal become very hard. For children in Sudan, this means it's not safe to go to school. Imagine not being able to learn and play with your friends every day! It's very difficult. Sometimes, families have to leave their homes because it's too dangerous to stay. They become 'refugees,' which means they have to find a new, safe place to live, often far away from everything they know. This is very scary for children and their parents. It also becomes hard to find yummy food to eat and clean water to drink. Hospitals, where doctors help people who are sick, might not be working properly. So, if someone gets hurt or feels unwell, it's very hard to get help. It’s like all the helpers in your town suddenly couldn't help anymore. Did you know? Before this sad fighting, children in Sudan loved to play football (soccer) and learn about their rich history!

What Can We Do to Help?

Even though we are far away, grown-ups all over the world are trying to help. They are trying to talk to the groups who are fighting to ask them to stop. They want them to put down their weapons and talk peacefully to solve their problems, just like we learn to do with our friends. Other kind people and groups are trying to send food, clean water, and medicine to the families who need it most. They want to make sure everyone has enough to eat and is healthy. It's like when your family helps a neighbor who is having a tough time. Every little bit of help is important. We can also remember the children of Sudan in our thoughts and wishes. We can wish for peace and safety for them. When we send kind thoughts, it shows we care, and caring is very powerful.

Why Peace is So Important

Peace means that everyone can be safe. It means children can go to school, play outside with their friends, and sleep soundly in their beds without being scared. It means families can stay together in their homes and grow food and live happily. Every child, everywhere in the world, deserves to grow up in peace. They deserve to laugh and learn and dream big dreams. The children of Sudan are just like you; they want to be happy and safe too. We hope the fighting stops soon so they can have that happiness and peace. Did you know? The River Nile brings life to many parts of Sudan, helping farmers grow food for their families.

My Wish for Sudan

So, my friends, that's the sad news from Sudan. But remember, there are many people working very hard to bring peace back. Let's all hope and wish that the grown-ups in Sudan stop fighting very soon. Let's wish for smiles and laughter to return to the faces of the children of Sudan. They deserve a world filled with kindness and peace, just like all of us. Let's send them our love!
summary: Our friend Aisha, a kid-friendly reporter, shares the sad news about a big fight called a 'conflict' happening in Sudan, a country in Africa, which makes life hard for children and families, but many people are trying to help bring peace.
whyMatters: This is important because when there's fighting, children can't go to school, families lose their homes, and it's hard to find food and water. We care about our friends in Sudan and hope for peace so they can be safe and happy again.

originalTitle: Trump voices support for possible Kurdish offensive in Iran
title: Trump voices support for possible Kurdish offensive in Iran
content: US president says he'd be 'all for' Kurdish ground assault on Iran amid reports that Washington is egging on rebellion.

content: Authorities say the mosque will be closed to worshippers, visitors of all faiths on Friday amid spread of the conflict.

title: US House of Representatives to vote on latest effort to halt Iran war
content: Vote comes day after a parallel resolution failed in the Senate, underscoring Republican support for Trump-launched war.

content: Iranian Kurdish opposition groups in exile in northern Iraq tell the BBC they have plans to cross the border but deny already doing so.

originalTitle: US asked Ukraine for help fighting Iranian drones, Zelensky says
title: US asked Ukraine for help fighting Iranian drones, Zelensky says
content: Ukraine's president says Kyiv will only help if doing so does not deplete its own air defences.

originalTitle: Israelis back war with Iran despite uncertainty and fatigue
title: Israelis back war with Iran despite uncertainty and fatigue
content: Israelis broadly support the US-Israeli military campaign with Iran, even if they are tired of air raid alerts and constant upheaval.

originalTitle: Forest tell Edu to stay away from training ground
title: Forest tell Edu to stay away from training ground
content: Nottingham Forest global head of football Edu is asked to stay away from the club's training ground.

originalTitle: Clubs vote in favour of six-team Championship play-off

content: EFL clubs approve plans to expand the Championship play-offs from four to six teams for the 2026-27 season onwards.

title: How relegation could cost Spurs more than £250m
